messages.content: Make an alternative timeline of Windows systems

1. Windows 1.0 (1985): Microsoft releases its first version of Windows, featuring a graphical user interface and simple applications.

2. Windows 2.0 (1987): The second version of Windows is released, with improved functionality and performance.

3. Windows 3.0 (1990): Microsoft introduces Windows 3.0, which includes new features such as improved graphics and multimedia support.

4. Windows NT (1993): Microsoft releases Windows NT as a separate operating system for business users, offering improved stability and security.

5. Windows 95 (1996): Microsoft releases Windows 95, which features a new user interface and improved functionality.

6. Windows 98 (1998): The next version of Windows is released, offering enhancements to performance and stability.

7. Windows 2000 (2000): Microsoft releases Windows 2000, targeting business users with improved networking and security features.

8. Windows XP (2002): Microsoft introduces Windows XP, which becomes one of the most popular operating systems worldwide.

9. Windows Vista (2006): Microsoft releases Windows Vista, featuring a new user interface and enhanced security features.

10. Windows 7 (2009): The next version of Windows is released, offering improved performance and compatibility with newer technologies.

11. Windows 8 (2012): Microsoft introduces Windows 8, featuring a new touch-optimized user interface and app store.

12. Windows 10 (2015): Microsoft releases Windows 10, offering a more integrated user experience across devices and improved security features.

13. Windows 11 (2021): Microsoft releases Windows 11, featuring a redesigned user interface and new productivity tools.
Update the names to planned names

1. Microsoft Interface Manager (1985)
2. Windows/286 (1987)
3. Windows/386 (1990)
4. Windows/NT OS/2 (1993)

8. Windows XP (2002)
9. Windows Longhorn (2006)
10. Vienna (2009)
11. Windows Blue (2012)
12. Windows 365 (2015)
13. Windows Sun Valley (2021)
